sourceMovie.title: West Side Story
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit West Side Story for the same reason it broke through in 2021: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Steven Spielberg keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Place in the Sun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If West Side Story clicked for you, A Place in the Sun (1951) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If West Side Story clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after West Side Story?
faq.answer: Try A Place in the Sun, American Psycho, Collateral—each shares DNA with West Side Story while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is West Side Story?
faq.answer: West Side Story (2021) is best described as Drama, Romance, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is West Side Story worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
